title: To The Same. On Looking Through Her Album.
content:
No wonder bards, both high and low,
        From Byron down to ***** and me,
    Should seek the fame which all bestow
        On him whose task is praising thee.

    Let but the theme be Jersey's eyes,
        At once all errors are forgiven;
    As even old Sternhold still we prize,
        Because, tho' dull, he sings of heaven.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Thomas Moore, titled "To The Same. On Looking Through Her Album.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
